calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-16-2021 Vacated

MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
Off Calendar Jun-02-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH DBA MERCY MEDICAL CENTER MERCED'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE.

Defendant Dignity Health's motion to change venue to Merced County under Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 397(c) is granted. Section 397(c) is discretionary and permits transfer from one proper county to another proper county when "the convenience of witnesses and the ends of justice would be promoted by the change." The burden is on the party seeking a change of venue to prove that both the convenience of witnesses and the ends of justice would be promoted by the change. (Corfee v. Southern California Edison Co. (1962) 202 Cal.App.2d 473, 477.) In this action, Plaintiff alleges that she suffered discrimination, harassment, and retaliation, among other wrongful actions, while working for Defendant Dignity Health in Merced, at Mercy Merced Medical Center. (First Amended Complaint ("FAC"); Schindler Decl., ISO Opp., Para. 2.)

Plaintiffs' opposition to this motion largely focuses on her argument that Defendant cannot rely on the convenience of its employees. However, even if the court does not consider the convenience of those employees, transfer would still promote the convenience of witnesses and the ends of justice. Plaintiff alleges that because of Defendant's actions she "suffered physical injury and became mentally upset, stressed and aggravated." (FAC Para. 41.) Defendant shows, based on Plaintiff's deposition testimony, that Plaintiff's treating healthcare providers and union witnesses as well as certain former employees largely reside or work in Merced County, that no pertinent events occurred in San Francisco, and Plaintiff is unaware of any potential witnesses who live there. (See Worley Decl., Paras. 5-7; Ex. 24; Arias Decl., Para. 7.) Defendant's showing, including the reasonable inferences from these facts, meets its burden to show that the convenience of witnesses would be promoted by a change in venue from San Francisco County to Merced County. (See Pearson v. Superior Court (1962) 199 Cal.App.2d 69, 79 ["it can be reasonably concluded that the ends of justice will be promoted by moving the trial to the county where all the witnesses reside"].) Plaintiff's claim that Defendant unreasonably delayed in filing its motion lacks merit.

Order is signed. .

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)
